stats.lessons: Nothing learned yet, since the campaign started in the middle of this period.

actuals.summary: arb will be airdropped to wallets that have recieved arb in loot boxes.

stats.changes: No meaningful changes happened in the last 2 weeks.
stats.lessons: The amount of lootboxes opened in the first days of the season is really high compared to the following days.

actuals.summary: Dispersed in future weeks through the planned verticals, loot boxes, leaderboards, battle pass rewards, and marketplace rebates.

planned.expectations: arb will be airdropped to wallets that have received arb in loot boxes

stats.changes: The only changes made from the initial application were a slight reorganization of ARB to lessen leaderboard rewards and increase Loot Box/Battle Pass rewards to help onboard more new and unique users instead of rewarding OG users who tend to dominate daily leaderboards.
stats.lessons: Rewarding arb in a more random way via lootboxes may bring more new users, versus competitive leaderboards that usually are dominated via OG players

actuals.summary: Incentives have been dispersed through the planned verticals, loot boxes, leaderboards, battle pass rewards, and marketplace rebates during the final week of Season 1 of our Play to Rip campaign. This dispersal also includes Season 1 marketplace rebates and Leaderboard rewards. These aforementioned verticals are dispersed only at the end of each of the three seasons.

planned.changes: The only changes made from the initial application were a slight reorganization of ARB to lessen leaderboard rewards and increase Loot Box/Battle Pass rewards to help onboard more new and unique users instead of rewarding OG users who tend to dominate daily leaderboards.
planned.expectations: Dependent upon RNG conditions in loot boxes but we are expecting to see a lesser amount as only 1.5 weeks will be incentivized to line up with the end of Play to Rip Season 1. Season 2 will begin in the two weeks following.

stats.changes: There were no big changes in this period.
stats.lessons: The game achieved a good maturity level, so the stability was the keyword of this period.

actuals.summary: Normal weeks of leaderboards and battlepass opening.

planned.changes: No changes planned for this period.
planned.expectations: Normal couple of weeks, with rewards to players mainly through lootboxes

stats.lessons: Every time a new season begin there is also a great increase in players, that will decrease along the season.

actuals.summary: Everything worked as planned and with the change from season 2 to season 3 we so a new increase in players, playing.

planned.changes: No changes for the next two weeks.
planned.expectations: With the end of Season 2 and the begin of the season 3, there will be a decrease in the playing time for the end of the season and a peak of new users in the begginning of season 3.

stats.lessons: Everything went as supposed, during the leaderboards some players, tried to exploid the game mechanics but were caught and removed from the leaderboards.

actuals.summary: Normal mid season weeks that had the start of the leaderboards in it.

planned.changes: No changes planned. This will be a mid season period, so normal weekly distributions based on the lootboxes opened
planned.expectations: That most of the lootboxes of the season will be opened in this period.

actuals.summary: In this phase we finalized the season 3 of our campaign and we wrapped up all the arb that we were able to give.
actuals.disclosures: Nothing special to disclose, unfortunatly we were not able to distribute all the arb.

planned.changes: There won't be changes to the plan.
planned.expectations: Finish the third and last season, and distribute all the arb possible.
